description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology to write, share work, and work with others, and can type at least two pages in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways with help from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child will write clearly and organize their ideas so they fit the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that follows the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that help others picture their experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to show the order of events in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child includes dialogue, descriptions, and pacing to show what happens and how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that relates to the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and special vocabulary to explain their topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas using words like 'especially' or 'in contrast' to help readers follow their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, examples, or quotes to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, shares main ideas, and organizes information logically, using headings or pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that relates to the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words like 'consequently' or 'specifically.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons in a clear order and supports them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, states their opinion, and organizes ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts with purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new long words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level skills to sound out and understand words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 4—5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging nonfiction texts on history, science, and technical subjects on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from several texts on the same topic to talk or write about it knowledgeably.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child explains how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses information from different sources to quickly find answers or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child compares different accounts of the same event, noticing important similarities and differences in viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child compares how two or more texts organize events or ideas, like by time order or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of important words related to grade 5 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child explains how people, events, or ideas are connected in a nonfiction text using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child finds two or more main ideas in a text, explains how details support them, and summarizes the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child quotes accurately from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4—5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child compares stories in the same genre by looking at how they handle similar themes and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, poem).
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures and multimedia add meaning, mood, or beauty to a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child describes how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view affects how events are told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child explains how chapters, scenes, or stanzas work together to create the whole story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when the author uses comparisons like metaphors and similes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how two or more characters, places, or events in a story are alike and different using details from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child understands the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem by looking at details and can summarize what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact quotes from a text to explain what it says and to make guesses about its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Science
description: Earth materials have different physical and chemical properties.
description_plain: Your child knows that Earth materials have different physical and chemical features.

subject: Science
description:
Earth materials provide resources for all living things, but these resources are limited and should be conserved.
This content standard is an application of the concepts in content standard 3.1 and 3.3 and should be integrated into one of these units.
description_plain: Your child understands that Earth materials give us resources we need, but these resources are limited and should be saved.

subject: Science
description: Organisms can survive and reproduce only in environments that meet their basic needs.
description_plain: Your child understands that animals and plants can only live and have babies in places that give them what they need to survive.

subject: Science
description: Materials have properties that can be identified and described through the use of simple tests.
description_plain: Your child can look at materials and use simple tests to describe what they are like.

subject: Science
description: An ecosystem is composed of all the populations that are living in a certain space and the physical factors with which they interact.
description_plain: Your child understands that an ecosystem includes all the living things in an area and the non-living things they interact with.

subject: Science
description:
Water moving across and through earth materials carries with it the products of human activities.
This content standard is an application of the concepts in content standard 6.3 and should be integrated into the same unit.
description_plain: Your child understands that water moving through the ground can carry things from human activities into the environment.

subject: Science
description: Variations in the amount of the sun's energy hitting the Earth's surface affect daily and seasonal weather patterns.
description_plain: Your child understands that changes in sunlight affect daily and seasonal weather.

subject: Science
description: Materials can be classified as pure substances or mixtures, depending on their chemical and physical properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort materials into pure substances or mixtures based on what they are made of and how they look.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when you divide a whole number by a unit fraction.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when you divide a unit fraction by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions, building on what they already know about division.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number (recognizing multiplication by whole numbers greater than 1 as a familiar case); explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number; and relating the principle of fraction equivalence a/b = (n×a)/(n×b) to the effect of multiplying a/b by 1.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying by a fraction bigger than 1 makes a number bigger, and multiplying by a fraction less than 1 makes it smaller, and understand multiplying fractions by 1 keeps them the same.

subject: Math
description: Comparing the size of a product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor, without performing the indicated multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can compare the size of a multiplication result to one factor by looking at the other factor without actually multiplying.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication as scaling (resizing), by:
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as resizing or scaling.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional side lengths by tiling it with unit squares of the appropriate unit fraction side lengths, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths. Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles, and represent fraction products as rectangular areas.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles with fractional side lengths by counting small squares and by multiplying the side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the product (a/b) × q as a parts of a partition of q into b equal parts; equivalently, as the result of a sequence of operations a × q ÷ b.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplying a fraction by a whole number as taking parts of that whole number or by multiplying and dividing in steps.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by another fraction or by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands a fraction as dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems using this idea.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions and whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators using pictures or equations and can estimate if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different denominators by changing them to have the same denominator first.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child uses equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes into groups based on their properties, like sides and angles.

subject: Math
description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape belongs to a big group, it also has the properties of smaller groups inside it.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can put flat shapes into categories by looking at their features.

subject: Math
description: Represent real world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane, and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph in the first quadrant and explain what the points mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines, called axes, to define a coordinate system, with the intersection of the lines (the origin) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates. Understand that the first number indicates how far to travel from the origin in the direction of one axis, and the second number indicates how far to travel in the direction of the second axis, with the convention that the names of the two axes and the coordinates correspond (e.g., x-axis and x-coordinate, y-axis and y-coordinate).
description_plain: Your child understands how to use two number lines that cross at zero to find points using pairs of numbers called coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can graph points on a coordinate plane to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive. Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made by joining two rectangular boxes by adding their volumes.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = b × h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole number sides in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base. Represent threefold whole-number products as volumes, e.g., to represent the associative property of multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a rectangular box by filling it with unit cubes and by multiplying its length, width, and height.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child understands volume relates to multiplication and addition and can solve problems about volume.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units.
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting unit cubes using different units like cubic centimeters or cubic inches.

subject: Math
description: A solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child knows that a solid made by fitting together n unit cubes has a volume of n cubic units.

subject: Math
description: A cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume, and can be used to measure volume.
description_plain: Your child understands a cube with sides one unit long is called a unit cube and has a volume of one cubic unit.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.
description_plain: Your child recognizes volume as a property of solid shapes and understands how to measure it.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child understands volume and how it connects to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements with fractions and use fraction math to solve problems from the plot.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system (e.g., convert 5 cm to 0.05 m), and use these conversions in solving multi-step, real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system and use these changes to solve multi-step problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child can convert between different units of measurement within the same system.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths using models and strategies, and explain how they solved the problems.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide whole numbers with up to four digits by two-digit numbers using place value and other strategies and explain their work.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can multiply multi-digit whole numbers quickly and accurately using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can do addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with multi-digit whole numbers and decimals to hundredths.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to any place value using their understanding of place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to thousandths and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form, e.g., 347.392 = 3 × 100 + 4 × 10 + 7 × 1 + 3 × (1/10) + 9 × (1/100) + 2 × (1/1000).
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals to thousandths in numbers, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare decimals up to thousandths.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child understands how multiplying or dividing by powers of 10 changes the number and decimal point, and can explain the patterns.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child knows that each digit in a number is ten times bigger than the digit to its right and one tenth the size of the digit to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how the place value system works.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns, and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns from rules, find relationships between them, and graph pairs of matching numbers.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.
description_plain: Your child can look at patterns and relationships between numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers, and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what the expressions mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use parentheses, brackets, or braces in numerical expressions, and evaluate expressions with these symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use parentheses, brackets, or braces in math expressions and solve problems that include these symbols.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write math expressions with numbers and explain what they mean.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and listen to others' ideas to decide if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems both in general ideas and with real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand problems and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Compose simple shapes to form larger shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put simple shapes together to make bigger shapes.

subject: Math
description: Model shapes in the world by building shapes from components (e.g., sticks and clay balls) and drawing shapes.
description_plain: Your child can build shapes using objects like sticks and clay and draw shapes they see around them.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and compare two- and three-dimensional shapes, in different sizes and orientations, using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts (e.g., number of sides and vertices/"corners") and other attributes (e.g., having sides of equal length).
description_plain: Your child can look at flat and solid shapes of different sizes and describe how they are alike and different, including parts like sides and corners.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.
description_plain: Your child can look at, compare, create, and put together different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional (lying in a plane, "flat") or three-dimensional ("solid").
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between flat shapes (2D) and solid shapes (3D).

subject: Math
description: Correctly name shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size.
description_plain: Your child can name shapes correctly no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes, and describe the relative positions of these objects using terms such as above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.
description_plain: Your child can name shapes they see and describe where things are using words like above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe shapes (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and talk about shapes like squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects into given categories; count the numbers of objects in each category and sort the categories by count.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups, count how many are in each group, and put the groups in order by size.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects and count the number of objects in each category.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into categories and count how many are in each category.

subject: Math
description: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has "more of"/"less of" the attribute, and describe the difference.
description_plain: Your child can compare two objects to see which one has more or less of something like length or weight and explain the difference.

subject: Math
description: Describe measurable attributes of objects, such as length or weight. Describe several measurable attributes of a single object.
description_plain: Your child can describe things about objects that can be measured, like how long or heavy they are, and talk about several of these features for one object.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can describe and compare things about objects that can be measured.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into ten ones and some further ones, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each composition or decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 18 = 10 + 8); understand that these numbers are composed of ten ones and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together numbers from 11 to 19 using groups of ten and extra ones, and show this with drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Work with numbers 11—19 to gain foundations for place value.
description_plain: Your child works with numbers 11 to 19 to understand how place value works.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 5.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 5.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record the answer with a drawing or equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the number that adds to another number from 1 to 9 to make 10, using objects or drawings, and write it down.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 5 = 2 + 3 and 5 = 4 + 1).
description_plain: Your child can break numbers up to 10 into pairs in different ways and show this with drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems, and add and subtract within 10, e.g., by using objects or drawings to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction story problems using objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds (e.g., claps), acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can show addition and subtraction using objects, fingers, pictures, sounds, acting, talking, or writing.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
description_plain: Your child understands that addition means putting things together and subtraction means taking things apart or away.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers from 1 to 10 written as numerals to see which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group, e.g., by using matching and counting strategies.
description_plain: Your child can tell if one group has more, less, or the same number of objects as another group by matching or counting.

subject: Math
description: Compare numbers.
description_plain: Compare numbers.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle, or as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration; given a number from 1—20, count out that many objects.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 things arranged in a line, rectangle, or circle, or up to 10 things scattered, and can count out a given number of objects up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.
description_plain: Your child understands that each number name you say when counting means one more than the number before.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.
description_plain: Your child knows that the last number said when counting tells how many objects there are, no matter how they are arranged or counted.

subject: Math
description: When counting objects, say the number names in the standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object.
description_plain: Your child counts objects by saying number names in order, matching one number to each object and one object to each number.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers and amounts are connected and that counting tells how many things there are.

subject: Math
description: Count to tell the number of objects.
description_plain: Your child can count to find out how many objects there are.

subject: Math
description: Write numbers from 0 to 20. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0-20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects).
description_plain: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20 and use these numbers to show how many objects there are, including zero for none.

subject: Math
description: Count forward beginning from a given number within the known sequence (instead of having to begin at 1).
description_plain: Your child can count forward starting from any number they know, not just from 1.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Know number names and the count sequence.
description_plain: Your child knows number names and the order of counting.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and listen to others' ideas to decide if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems both in general ideas and with real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand problems and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Relating the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can choose the best way to describe data based on its shape and the situation it came from.

subject: Math
description: Giving quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (interquartile range and/or mean absolute deviation), as well as describing any overall pattern and any striking deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain numbers that show the center and spread of data and describe patterns or unusual points.

subject: Math
description: Describing the nature of the attribute under investigation, including how it was measured and its units of measurement.
description_plain: Your child can describe what is being measured in data, how it was measured, and the units used.

subject: Math
description: Reporting the number of observations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how many data points were collected.

subject: Math
description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context, such as by:
description_plain: Your child can summarize sets of numbers by explaining what they mean in their real-life context.

subject: Math
description: Display numerical data in plots on a number line, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots.
description_plain: Your child can show number data on number lines using dot plots, histograms, and box plots.

subject: Math
description: Summarize and describe distributions.
description_plain: Your child can summarize and describe how data is spread out and shaped.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a measure of center for a numerical data set summarizes all of its values with a single number, while a measure of variation describes how its values vary with a single number.
description_plain: Your child understands that one number can show the center of data and another number can show how much the data varies.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution which can be described by its center, spread, and overall shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that data collected to answer a question has a pattern that can be described by its center, spread, and shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers.
description_plain: Your child knows a statistical question expects different answers because the data can vary.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of statistical variability.
description_plain: Your child learns about how data can vary and what that means.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable. Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to show how two changing amounts are related, write equations for these, and understand the relationship by looking at graphs and tables.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can show and study how one number changes when another number changes.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities of the form x > c or x < c have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number line diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities like x > c or x < c to show limits in problems and draw their solutions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form x + p = q and px = q for cases in which p, q and x are all nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems by writing and solving simple equations like x + p = q or px = q with positive numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving a real-world or mathematical problem; understand that a variable can represent an unknown number, or, depending on the purpose at hand, any number in a specified set.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers and write expressions to solve problems, understanding that letters can mean unknown or any number in a group.

subject: Math
description: Understand solving an equation or inequality as a process of answering a question: which values from a specified set, if any, make the equation or inequality true? Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.
description_plain: Your child understands that solving an equation or inequality means finding which numbers make it true and can check this by plugging in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Reason about and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can think through and solve equations and inequalities that have one variable.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent (i.e., when the two expressions name the same number regardless of which value is substituted into them).
description_plain: Your child can tell when two expressions are equal because they give the same result no matter what number is used.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to create expressions that mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate expressions at specific values of their variables. Include expressions that arise from formulas used in real-world problems. Perform arithmetic operations, including those involving whole-number exponents, in the conventional order when there are no parentheses to specify a particular order (Order of Operations).
description_plain: Your child can find the value of expressions when numbers are plugged in, including using exponents and following the correct order of operations.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient); view one or more parts of an expression as a single entity.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of an expression like sum, product, factor, and coefficient, and see parts of an expression as one piece.

subject: Math
description: Write expressions that record operations with numbers and with letters standing for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write expressions that show math operations using numbers and letters for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and find the value of expressions where letters stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and calculate expressions that include whole-number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to work with expressions that include variables.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by plotting points in all four parts of the coordinate plane and use coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between comparing absolute values and comparing which number is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Understand the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line; interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in a real-world situation.
description_plain: Your child understands absolute value as how far a number is from zero on the number line and can explain this in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain order statements about rational numbers in real-world examples.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statements of inequality as statements about the relative position of two numbers on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can understand inequalities as showing where numbers are positioned compared to each other on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how to order rational numbers and knows what absolute value means.

subject: Math
description: Find and position integers and other rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram; find and position pairs of integers and other rational numbers on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and place integers and rational numbers on number lines and on coordinate planes.

subject: Math
description: Understand signs of numbers in ordered pairs as indicating locations in quadrants of the coordinate plane; recognize that when two ordered pairs differ only by signs, the locations of the points are related by reflections across one or both axes.
description_plain: Your child understands that the signs in ordered pairs show where points are on the coordinate plane and can see how points reflect across axes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize opposite signs of numbers as indicating locations on opposite sides of 0 on the number line; recognize that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself, e.g., -(-3) = 3, and that 0 is its own opposite.
description_plain: Your child knows that opposite signs mean numbers are on opposite sides of zero and that the opposite of the opposite number is the original number.

subject: Math
description: Understand a rational number as a point on the number line. Extend number line diagrams and coordinate axes familiar from previous grades to represent points on the line and in the plane with negative number coordinates.
description_plain: Your child understands that rational numbers can be shown as points on number lines and coordinate planes, including negative numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values (e.g., temperature above/below zero, elevation above/below sea level, credits/debits, positive/negative electric charge); use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real-world contexts, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation.
description_plain: Your child knows that positive and negative numbers describe things going in opposite directions or having opposite values and can explain what zero means in these situations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to the system of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about numbers to understand and work with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100 and the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12. Use the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers 1—100 with a common factor as a multiple of a sum of two whole numbers with no common factor.
description_plain: Your child can find the greatest common factor of two numbers up to 100 and the least common multiple of two numbers up to 12, and use these to rewrite sums in simpler forms.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit decimals using the standard algorithm for each operation.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals with many digits using standard methods.

subject: Math
description: Fluently divide multi-digit numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly divide large numbers using the usual steps.

subject: Math
description: Compute fluently with multi-digit numbers and find common factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child can work quickly with large numbers and find common factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions, and solve word problems involving division of fractions by fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate division of fractions by fractions and solve related word problems using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio reasoning to convert measurement units; manipulate and transform units appropriately when multiplying or dividing quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use ratio thinking to change measurement units and correctly adjust units when multiplying or dividing.

subject: Math
description: Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g., 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity); solve problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percent.
description_plain: Your child can find a percent of a number by thinking of it as parts per 100 and solve problems where they find the whole from a part and a percent.

subject: Math
description: Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about unit rates, like figuring out prices per item or speeds.

subject: Math
description: Make tables of equivalent ratios relating quantities with whole number measurements, find missing values in the tables, and plot the pairs of values on the coordinate plane. Use tables to compare ratios.
description_plain: Your child can make tables of equivalent ratios with whole numbers, find missing numbers, plot these on graphs, and use tables to compare ratios.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems, e.g., by reasoning about tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can use ratio and rate thinking to solve real-life and math problems using tables, diagrams, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a:b with b ≠ 0, and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.
description_plain: Your child understands what a unit rate is and can use words to describe rates in ratio relationships.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.
description_plain: Your child understands what a ratio is and can use words to explain the relationship between two amounts.

subject: Math
description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands ratios and can use ratio thinking to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns made of rectangles and triangles that fold into 3D shapes, and use these patterns to find the total surface area of the shapes, solving real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices; use coordinates to find the length of a side joining points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can plot shapes on a grid using points and find the length of sides when points share the same x or y coordinate, applying this to solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes of the appropriate unit fraction edge lengths, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths of the prism. Apply the formulas V = l w h and V = b h to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a box-shaped figure with fractional side lengths by filling it with smaller cubes and by multiplying the side lengths, using formulas to solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes; apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special four-sided shapes, and polygons by breaking them into rectangles or triangles, and use these methods to solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve finding area, surface area, and volume in real-life and math situations.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and listen to others' ideas to decide if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems both in general ideas and with real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand problems and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., because).
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including words like 'because' to show simple ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words and describing words by explaining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life things, like noticing cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can explain words by their group and important features, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups like colors or clothes to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child can understand how words relate to each other and different meanings of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common root words and their different forms, like 'look,' 'looks,' and 'looking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common beginnings and endings of words to help figure out what the words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from sentences to understand the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell new words by sounding them out and using spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly, including some irregular words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas in dates and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child can capitalize names of people and dates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand complete sentences that tell, ask, command, or show strong feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child can use common words that show location or time, like 'during' or 'toward.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'a,' 'the,' and 'this' to point out things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child can use common connecting words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words to add details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child can use verbs to talk about things that happened before, now, or will happen later.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their; anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child can use words like I, me, my, they, them, their, anyone, and everything correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child can use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns, names of people or places, and show possession.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and sentence structure when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child will say complete sentences when it fits the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events with clear details and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand what someone else is saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to clear up confusion about topics or stories being discussed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
